message:
🐛 fix(e2e): four hardcoded-values defects across the test suite (Issues 9240, 9241, 9242, 9243) (#9252)

Bundles four khushiiagrawal-reported test-quality defects that all share
the same root cause class: hardcoded values in test files that either
diverge from canonical config or get ignored entirely, so regressions
the tests were meant to catch slip through silently.

### Issue 9240 — cluster-investigation.spec.ts ignores its own load budget

The test imports assertLoadTime and defines CLUSTER_LOAD_MAX_MS = 3_000,
but only asserts body visibility + textContent length. A clusters page
taking 10s to load would still pass. Wire the helper in.

### Issue 9241 — card-drag-and-reorder.spec.ts guesses localStorage keys

Reads card order from three speculative key names ('kubestellar-card-order',
'dashboard-card-order', 'card-layout') — none of which match the
canonical STORAGE_KEY_MAIN_DASHBOARD_CARDS = 'kubestellar-main-dashboard-cards'
exported by src/lib/constants/storage.ts. If all three missed, orderBefore
was null and the test silently passed even when persistence was broken.
Import the canonical constant; assert non-null before reload so a
stop-writing regression fails loudly.

### Issue 9242 — security-compliance.spec.ts hardcodes the wrong port

BASE_URL fallback was 'http://localhost:5174' (Vite dev server) but
playwright.config.ts:72 defaults to 'http://localhost:8080' (the Go
backend that serves both API and built frontend). Without
PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL set, multi-page security checks (lines 707-751) and
the auth-bypass check (line 772) navigated to the wrong port and
silently failed. Match the project default.

### Issue 9243 — i18n-compliance.spec.ts hardcodes the locale namespace list

['common.json', 'cards.json', 'status.json', 'errors.json'] is fixed in
the test, so any new namespace under src/locales/en/ (e.g., missions.json,
ai.json) is silently ignored — missing-key, empty-value, and plural-form
checks would not apply. Replace with fs.readdirSync(localeDir).filter
(f => f.endsWith('.json')) so additions are automatically covered. The
list happens to match the directory contents at filing time; this is
proactive against drift.

### Why bundled

All four issues:
  - filed by @khushiiagrawal in the same 2026-04-20 batch
  - touch web/e2e/ files exclusively
  - share the 'hardcoded value should be config-driven or actually used'
    root cause class
  - are small, scoped fixes (no new files, no test restructuring)
  - close cleanly with separate Fixes # trailers

Closing four issues in one PR is more efficient than four separate PRs
that all touch web/e2e/ in non-conflicting ways.

Fixes #9240
Fixes #9241
Fixes #9242
Fixes #9243

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -184,7 +184,15 @@ test('i18n compliance — internationalization audit', async ({ page }) => {
   console.log('[i18n] Phase 1: Locale file validation')
 
   const localeDir = path.resolve(__dirname, '../../src/locales/en')
-  const localeFiles = ['common.json', 'cards.json', 'status.json', 'errors.json']
+  // Issue 9243: previously this list was hardcoded as
+  // ['common.json', 'cards.json', 'status.json', 'errors.json']. Any new
+  // namespace under src/locales/en/ (e.g., missions.json, ai.json) was
+  // silently ignored — missing-key validation, empty-value checks, and
+  // plural-form checks would not apply. Discover namespaces by scanning
+  // the directory so additions are automatically covered.
+  const localeFiles = fs.readdirSync(localeDir)
+    .filter(f => f.endsWith('.json'))
+    .sort()
 
   let totalKeys = 0
   let emptyValues = 0

@@ -36,7 +36,15 @@ interface SecurityReport {
 
 const IS_CI = !!process.env.CI
 const CI_TIMEOUT_MULTIPLIER = 2
-const BASE_URL = process.env.PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:5174'
+// Issue 9242: previous fallback was 'http://localhost:5174' (Vite dev
+// server), which diverges from playwright.config.ts:72 which uses
+// 'http://localhost:8080' (the Go backend that serves both API and the
+// built frontend). When the env var was unset, multi-page security
+// checks at lines 707-751 and the auth-bypass check at line 772
+// navigated to the wrong port and silently failed/skipped. Match the
+// project default so explicit-URL navigations work in both
+// configurations.
+const BASE_URL = process.env.PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:8080'
 
 // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 // Helpers

@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@ import {
   NETWORK_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS,
 } from '../helpers/setup'
 import { assertTouchTargetSize } from '../helpers/ux-assertions'
+import { STORAGE_KEY_MAIN_DASHBOARD_CARDS } from '../../src/lib/constants/storage'
 
 /**
  * Card drag-and-reorder UX tests.
@@ -92,30 +93,37 @@ test.describe('Card Drag and Reorder', () => {
       return
     }
 
-    // Capture current card order from localStorage
-    const orderBefore = await page.evaluate(() => {
-      return localStorage.getItem('kubestellar-card-order')
-        || localStorage.getItem('dashboard-card-order')
-        || localStorage.getItem('card-layout')
-        || null
-    })
+    // Issue 9241: previously this test guessed at three speculative
+    // localStorage keys ('kubestellar-card-order', 'dashboard-card-order',
+    // 'card-layout') — none of which match the canonical key. If all three
+    // missed, orderBefore was null and the test silently passed even when
+    // the app didn't persist card order at all. Use the exported key from
+    // src/lib/constants/storage.ts so a renaming refactor on the source
+    // breaks this test (intended), and so the test actually exercises the
+    // persistence path.
+    const orderBefore = await page.evaluate(
+      (key) => localStorage.getItem(key),
+      STORAGE_KEY_MAIN_DASHBOARD_CARDS,
+    )
+
+    // Persistence is only meaningful if the card-order hook has written
+    // something. Without it, the test would pass vacuously — make the
+    // failure visible instead of silently annotating.
+    expect(
+      orderBefore,
+      `Expected localStorage key "${STORAGE_KEY_MAIN_DASHBOARD_CARDS}" to be populated by ` +
+      `the card-order persistence hook before reload. If the hook stopped writing, this is a regression.`,
+    ).not.toBeNull()
 
     await page.reload()
     await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle', { timeout: NETWORK_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS }).catch(() => {})
 
-    const orderAfter = await page.evaluate(() => {
-      return localStorage.getItem('kubestellar-card-order')
-        || localStorage.getItem('dashboard-card-order')
-        || localStorage.getItem('card-layout')
-        || null
-    })
-
-    // If there is a persisted order, it should survive reload
-    if (orderBefore !== null) {
-      expect(orderAfter).toBe(orderBefore)
-    } else {
-      test.info().annotations.push({ type: 'ux-finding', description: 'No card order found in localStorage — order may not persist' })
-    }
+    const orderAfter = await page.evaluate(
+      (key) => localStorage.getItem(key),
+      STORAGE_KEY_MAIN_DASHBOARD_CARDS,
+    )
+
+    expect(orderAfter).toBe(orderBefore)
   })
 
   test('touch targets on drag handles meet 44px minimum', async ({ page }) => {
